text: The patient is positioned in lateral decubitus. Three trocars are inserted along the midline: two 12mm and one 5mm, with an additional 12mm subcostal trocar. The surgeon releases the lower border of the liver and identifies the inferior vena cava.

The surgeon dissects the right renal artery at the intercaval aortic level and applies early clamping. Four hepatic veins, tributaries of the caudate lobe, are identified on the anterior face of the inferior vena cava, and are ligated and sectioned.

The surgeon progresses above the upper limit of the thrombus in the inferior vena cava and places a Rommel's tourniquet around the vessel. The tourniquet is then adjusted, and the vascular clamps are removed.
